column1: Imagine being a pregnant woman in a bustling city in Africa. You work long hours at a demanding job
column2: often in uncomfortable conditions. You have limited time for rest and self-care
column3: and you worry about the impact of your pregnancy on your career. On top of that
column4: you're concerned about eating healthy
column5: and accessing on-demand medical support especially with your busy schedule.

column1: This is the reality for millions of working women in Africa. They face unique challenges in balancing their professional responsibilities with the demands of pregnancy. The long hours
column2: stress
column3: and exposure to potential hazards in the workplace can increase the risk of pregnancy complications. Additionally
column4: many working women may not have access to adequate medical support or flexible work arrangements
column5: making it difficult to prioritize their health and well-being.

column1: Limited access to quality maternal healthcare further compounds the challenges faced by working women. In many urban areas
column2: healthcare facilities may be overcrowded
column3: leading to long waiting times. This can make it difficult for pregnant women to access timely and appropriate care. For example
column4: a recent study found that the average waiting time for prenatal care in some African countries is over four hours.

column1: Furthermore
column2: the demand for multiple  hospital visits can be a significant burden for working women
column3: who may already be struggling to meet their career goals. A lack of convenient healthcare can force women to delay or forego essential prenatal check-ups
column4: increasing their risk of complications.

column1: The consequences of limited access to maternal healthcare can be devastating for working women. They are at a higher risk of complications
column2: including preterm birth
column3: low birth weight
column4: and pregnancy-induced hypertension. These complications can have a significant impact on both the mother and the baby
column5: and can lead to long-term health consequences. According to the World Health Organisation
column6: 830 women die every day from preventable pregnancy complications.

column1: Additionally
column2: working women  face challenges in maintaining a healthy diet during pregnancy. Long working hours
column3: limited access to nutritious food
column4: and the cost of healthy food can contribute to malnutrition
column5: anaemia
column6: and food poisoning. These nutritional deficiencies can have serious consequences for both the mother and the baby
column7: increasing the risk of complications and poor birth outcomes.

column1: By addressing these challenges
column2: we can help to reduce maternal mortality rates in Africa and ensure that every working woman has the opportunity to experience a safe and healthy pregnancy and childbirth.

SunWell Africa has made a significant impact on the lives of pregnant women and their families. We managed 15 women with preeclampsia
column3: a serious condition that can lead to complications for both mother and baby. I'm incredibly proud to say that all 15 women and their babies had successful outcomes. This is a great outcome when compared to the general population where 5%-7% of deaths happen due to preeclampsia.

column1: We've also seen a remarkable improvement in the health of our users' children. Thanks to our pregnancy classes and personalized care
column2: we've reduced the incidence of diarrhea by 90% and eradicated diarrheal-related death totally.. That means fewer sick children and healthier families.

column1: ;;TrueNorth Impact provides a product solution and distribution solution to reach the lower income rural population that is hardest affected by the lack of access to safe
column2: affordable drinking water.

column1: 1)	Our product:
TrueNorth Impact consist of two 16-liter foodgrade (PP5) containers placed on top of each other
column2: with a ceramic membrane placed on the bottom of the upper container. The process begins by pouring unsafe water into the upper container. Gravity pushes the dirty water through the TrueNorth Impact Prot3ct
column3: a ceramic membrane.

column1: Water is purified through a three-stage filtration process which ensures high-quality water purification
column2: with a 99.9% effective bacterial removal and improved taste and odor. The filtration process is as follows:

column1: 1.	A ceramic membrane with pores of 0.4 micron to block bacteria
column2: dirt and sediments from the water;

column1: 2.	Nanosilver to kill bacteria and other micro-organisms;
3.	Activated carbon to reduce the chemical content and improve the taste
column2: smell and odor color of the water.

column1: According to the WHO test report
column2: the TrueNorth Impact Prot3ct has a 99
column3: 9% effective bacterial removal.

column1: As the water flows through the ceramic membrane
column2: bacteria
column3: impurities and dirt are removed. The filtered water is then collected in the lower container (16L)
column4: where it is safely stored in a sealed container preventing recontamination. A tap attached to the lower container allows users to drink the safe and clean water directly.

column1: These technologies are integrated into a user-friendly design with easy maintenance. The filter package includes a brush for cleaning the ceramic membrane in case it gets clogged and an indicator to monitor the filter’s lifespan of about 3 years. The TrueNorth Impact ceramic membrane can purify up to 7
column2: 000 liters and lasts up to three years; making it one of the best tested
column3: most durable and most affordable water filters currently available on the Kenyan market. The ceramic membrane only costs US$20. There is no other solution currently available on the market that caters for safe drinking water for less than US$4/year.

column1: 2)	Distribution:

TrueNorth Impact targets lower income rural households that live of less then US$7 per day. Unfortunately these households can’t afford to pay US$ 50 cash-upfront. That’s why we partner with Micro Finance Institutes (MFIs). The MFIs have the same target market and mission alignment as TrueNorth Impact. They target women groups that use social collateral to guarantee for the outstanding loans of the group members. The TrueNorth Impact Safe Water Expert (Sales agent -SWE) follows the MFIs loan officer visiting these women groups and after the loan modalities have been discussed the SWE talks about the importance of health and safe drinking water. Anyone interested in purchasing a TrueNorth Impact Water Filter can register for a loan through the MFI. Once the loan is approved we ship the product within 48 hours to the customer. The MFI pays us at the end of the month for all products delivered and the client pays back to the MFI in a period between 6-12 months. Through these partnerships we directly reach our target market and enable them to pay in installments. Whilst outsourcing the installment collections to the MFIs.

column1: •	Self –contained system: The AquaVantage Group eco toilet is a self-contain unit

column2: which Nia is becoming. Hygiene and reproductive health are at the core of our work. Through our curriculum girls can remember at least one form of modern contraceptives – how powerful would it be if the Nia brand could support awareness of VivaCare and purchases of products for reduced risk of STDs and unwanted pregnancy. Our HealthLink supports adolescents at the onset of puberty through all life’s major milestones. Our focus has had to be on consent and saying no to unwanted sex as 95% of girls’ first sexual encounter is unwanted. They often rarely use contraceptives as well – we have often discussed the next step to be more sex-positive for adults (which VivaCare does well) and created an adult comic about contraceptives which takes place in a bar – which perhaps we could refresh and relaunch together. We could have branded content on health and hygiene brought to people on our HealthLink by VivaCare
